data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Falter as Trump Plans 50% Tariffs on EU, 25% on Foreign Smartphones
data.description:
The stock market has finished another eventful week under the Trump presidency amid new tariff threats and an impending trade agreement.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

"Our discussions with them are going nowhere!," said Trump in regard to U.S.-EU trade talks this morning alongside a new plan to levy the 27-nation bloc with 50% tariffs, effective as of June 1. Trump added that the EU was formed in order to take advantage of the U.S. on trade, stressing that it was "difficult to deal with" them.

Alongside the EU tariff announcement was a warning for Apple (AAPL). Trump said that he would hit the company's iPhones with a 25% tariff if production of the devices aren't moved to the U.S. from India or anywhere else. Later on, Trump said that the warning doesn't only apply to Apple but to all smartphone makers who manufacture their devices outside of the U.S. That would include Samsung and Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) as well.

In terms of more positive trade developments, the U.S. and India could announce the first tranche of an in principle agreement within seven to 10 days, reported CNBC TV18, a CNBC affiliate based in India. The agreement would exempt some items from the U.S.' reciprocal tariffs while keeping a 10% tariff on all other imports to the U.S.

The potential tariff deal aligns with comments made by Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Friday. Bessent said that trade talks with India and other Asian countries are "far along." Bessent added that he expects numerous trade deals to be signed before the 90-day reciprocal tariff pause expires.

The S&P 500 (SPX) finished the week with a 1.72% loss while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 1.19%.

data.title: Trump Touts Pennsylvania's 'Largest Investment' in History
data.description:
President Trump has announced a "planned partnership" between Japan's Nippon Steel and U.S. Steel (X), which will result in the "largest Investment in the History of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania." Back in December 2023, Nippon announced that it would acquire U.S. Steel for $14.9 billion, although the deal was put on hold by former President Joe Biden given national security and supply chain concerns.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

Nippon Steel and U.S. Steel to Form a Planned Partnership

Trump said that the partnership will create at least 70,000 jobs and add $14 billion to the U.S. economy. In the meantime, U.S. Steel's headquarters will remain in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania.

X stock closed higher by 21.31% on the news.

data.title: Trump Floats 25% Tariff for all Non-U.S. Smartphone Makers, Not Just Apple
data.description:
Companies that produce smartphones, such as Samsung and Google (GOOG) and (GOOGL), could be subject to a 25% tariff on the devices if they don't move its production to the U.S., said Trump on Friday in the Oval Office.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

This morning, the President warned that Apple (AAPL) iPhones could be charged a 25% tariff if production of the devices aren't moved to the U.S. from India.

Trump Targets Smartphone Makers

Both Samsung and Google produce their smartphones in various locations in Asia, such as Vietnam and India. Transitioning production to the U.S. would be a timely and costly process and could result in more expensive prices for customers given higher labor costs in the U.S.

Bank of America analyst Wamsi Mohan estimates that the price of a "Made in the USA" iPhone 16 Pro would increase by 25% to about $1,500 from $1,199 when only considering the increase in labor costs. Wedbush's Dan Ives estimates that a domestically produced iPhone could jump to $3,500.

AAPL stock is sinking lower by nearly 3% on the news.

data.title: NY Fed Expects 2.4% GDP Growth for Q2
data.description:
The New York Fed now expects U.S. gross domestic product (GDP) to grow by 2.43% during the second quarter, up from its estimate of 2.35% a week ago. The estimate is slightly higher than the St. Louis Fed's prediction of 2.34% GDP growth.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

Positive GDP growth is essential for the second quarter, as GDP fell by 0.30% during the first quarter. Historically, a recession has loosely been defined as two consecutive quarters of declining GDP. Rising GDP is also a positive signal for the stock market as it often correlates with corporate earnings growth.

NY Fed Cites a Positive Housing Data Surprise

The NY Fed said that a "positive surprise from housing data drove the increase." This is likely a reference to a seasonally-adjusted annual rate of 743,000 new single-family home sales in April, above the expectation for 695,000 sales and increasing by 10.9% month-over-month.

data.title: Nasdaq 100 Index Today: QQQ Drops amid EU 50% Tariff Threat, Potential U.S-India Trade Deal
data.description:
The Nasdaq 100 (NDX) opened Friday morning with a 1.44% loss as trade deal fear and optimism returns to the spotlight.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

Before the market open, President Trump said that he would recommend a 50% tariff on the EU given that trade negotiations "are going nowhere!" Trump added that the U.S. has a significant trade deficit with the bloc and that it was created to take advantage of U.S. trade. Trump's tariff threat is on a "completely different order of magnitude," said Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee on CNBC's "Squawk Box."

On a more positive note, CNBC TV18 reported that the U.S. and India could sign a trade agreement within seven to 10 days. The deal will reportedly include an exemption of some Indian products from the U.S.' 26% reciprocal tariffs while some other products would incur zero duty charges. Any remaining Indian imports would be subject to a 10% tariff.

The Nasdaq 100 has staged a partial recovery from its morning drop and is down by 0.52% at the time of writing.

Which Stocks are Moving the Nasdaq 100?

Let's take a look at TipRank's Nasdaq 100 Heatmap, which illustrates the stocks that have contributed to the index's price action.

Apple (AAPL) is a big loser on the day after Trump threatened to place a 25% tariff on the company if it doesn't bring iPhone production to the U.S. from India. Apple is just one of many tech companies in the red, joined by semiconductor firms Texas Instruments (TXN) and Qualcomm (QCOM) as the global AI race marches on.

Within consumer cyclicals, Amazon (AMZN) is falling lower, although not as much as MercadoLibre (MELI), which is still digesting an announcement that its CEO will step down in 2026. Meanwhile, most consumer defensive stocks are in the green given increasing economic and tariff uncertainty.

QQQ Stock Moves Lower with the Nasdaq 100

The Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) is an exchange-traded fund (ETF) designed to track the movement of the Nasdaq 100. As a result, QQQ is falling alongside the Nasdaq 100 today.

Wall Street is quite bullish on QQQ stock. During the past three months, analysts have issued an average QQQ price target of $573.68 for the stocks within the index, implying upside of 12.20% from current prices. The 102 stocks in the QQQ carry 87 buy ratings, 15 hold ratings, and zero sell ratings.

data.title: Bessent Says the U.S. will Reach a Equilibrium Rate for Tariffs
data.description:
With tariff fears back in the spotlight, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has issued a new comment on the extent of duties. "There's an equilibrium rate, what's called a Laffer curve, for tariffs. I think we will reach that rate," said Bessent in a Bloomberg interview.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks straight to you inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

What is the Laffer Curve?

The Laffer curve for tariffs refers to a specific tariff rate that will maximize government revenue. Any rate above this rate could actually reduce revenue given the effects of tariffs on trade and the economy.

Bessent also noted that non-tariff trade barriers from U.S. partners have fallen lower since President Trump's tariffs were enacted. Furthermore, he expects the U.S. to generate several hundred billion dollars a year from tariffs, which will result in the reduction of bond issuance in the same amount.

data.title: France "Ready to respond" to Trump's EU Tariff Threat
data.description:
Following President Trump's threat to tariff the EU at a 50% rate, France's Minister Delegate for Foreign Trade, Laurent Saint-Martin, said on X in a translated post that France maintains its stance of de-escalation but is "ready to respond." France is a member of the 27-nation EU.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

U.S-EU Tensions Rise amid 50% Tariff Threat

Saint-Martin added that the threat isn't conducive to trade discussions between the U.S. and EU and that he would appear on a French TV channel to discuss the development in a few hours.

The U.S. has a $16.38 billion goods trade deficit with France, exporting $43.52 billion of goods and importing $59.90 billion of goods in 2024.

The S&P 500 (SPX) is falling lower on Friday as trade and tariff tensions return to the spotlight.

data.title: Bessent Says U.S. 'Far Along' in India, Asian Countries Trade Talks
data.description:
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has said that the U.S. is "far along" in trade negotiations with India and Asian countries in an interview with Fox News this morning. While Bessent didn't specify the exact Asian countries, he added that he expects trade deals to be announced before the end of the 90-day reciprocal tariff pause.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

Bessent Hopes Trump 50% Tariff Threat will 'Light a fire under the EU'

However, unlike the progress made with other U.S. trading partners, the EU is an "exception," said Bessent. This morning, Trump threatened 50% tariffs on the 27-nation bloc, saying that U.S.-EU trade discussions "are going nowhere!"

"I would hope that this would light a fire under the EU," said Bessent, explaining that the threat was a response to the EU's pace of material trade talks.

data.title: U.S.-India Could Announce Trade Agreement within 10 Days
data.description:
The U.S. and India have made steady progress in trade talks and could announce the first tranche of an in principle agreement within seven to 10 days, sources told CNBC TV18. The deal would exempt some Indian products from the U.S.' 26% reciprocal tariffs with other products receiving zero duty fees. All other Indian imports to the U.S. would be charged with a 10% tariff.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

Bessent Says U.S. is 'Far Along' in India Trade Talks

The update aligns with U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's comment this morning that the U.S. is "far along" in trade talks with India.

In 2024, the U.S. exported $41.8 billion of goods to India and imported $87.4 billion, resulting in a goods trade deficit of $45.7 billion.

The S&P 500 (SPX) is trading higher on the news after opening lower this morning in response to Trump's 50% tariff threat on the EU.

data.title: 50% EU Tariffs 'Completely different order of magnitude,' says Fed's Goolsbee
data.description:
This morning, President Trump proposed a 50% tariff on the EU, adding that the 27-nation bloc was created in order to take "advantage of the United States on TRADE." In response, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee noted that "To go to 10% was going to be the highest tariff rate that we had on the world in 90 years. To go to 50% is a completely different order of magnitude" on CNBC's "Squawk Box."

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks right to your inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

Trump Tariff Threat "Really Scary" for Supply Chains

Goolsbee also added that the tariff threat would be "really scary" for supply chains. "Maybe this is just a stepping stone and that on the back-end of this thing we can get some new deals," suggested the Fed President.

In 2024, the U.S. exported $370.2 billion of goods to the EU and imported $605.8 billion, resulting in a goods trade deficit of $235.6 billion.

The S&P 500 (SPX) is sinking on Trump's tariff threat and is down by 0.99%.
